name: Astrakhan Oblast (Russia)

colors.symbolism: the Volga, the Caspian Sea, and the historic Astrakhan Khanate
the gold of the Tsar's crown and the steppes

description: A blue field bearing a yellow royal crown above a white scimitar with point to the hoist, drawn from the historic coat of arms of Astrakhan.
history: Adopted on 27 December 2001 by the State Duma of the Astrakhan Oblast. The design recalls the coat of arms of the Astrakhan Tsardom (16th-18th centuries) which itself memorialised the conquest of the Astrakhan Khanate by Ivan IV in 1556.

features.symbolism: the blue field draws on the historic banner of the Astrakhan Khanate and recalls the waters of the Caspian Sea and the Volga River.
the historic crown of the Astrakhan Khanate, taken into the imperial Russian regalia after the conquest of Astrakhan in 1556.
an Eastern scimitar (silver sabre) beneath the crown; recalls the steppe-warrior heritage of the region.
